page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the t2d server family. It features 48 dedicated physical cores and 48 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor using the x86_64 architecture. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each vCPU maps to a dedicated physical core. The system is equipped with 192.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no bundled local NVMe storage or GPU hardware included with this instance type.

In benchmark evaluations, the t2d-standard-48 demonstrates strong multi-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core tests and the top 25% for Geekbench multi-core and Passmark CPU tests. Single-core performance is rated as strong in stress-ng and average in Geekbench. Memory performance is mixed, showing strong read-only bandwidth for small cached blocks but average bandwidth for larger uncached blocks. Cryptographic and integer operations are supported by hardware flags including AES, VAES, SHA_NI, BMI2, and ADX, contributing to strong performance in Passmark encryption and database operations.

The instance is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads such as software compilation, image processing, ray tracing, and database operations, all of which score in the top 25% in benchmarks. It also handles light to medium language model inference efficiently, though performance drops to the bottom 25% for text generation on large 70B models. Given its dedicated core allocation and balanced resource density, it offers a highly capable environment for general-purpose compute, web serving, and Redis deployments without the overhead of hyperthreading.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose server featuring 48 dedicated vCPUs and 48 physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, it provides 192.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng multi-core tests, alongside strong results in software compilation, ray tracing, and database operations. It exhibits average memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks and average single-core performance. This hardware profile is optimized for multi-threaded workloads, database operations, Redis deployments, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 is a general-purpose server with 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 192 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-48 features 48 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 192 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A dedicated 48-core AMD EPYC platform with balanced memory allocation, delivering top-tier multi-threaded performance for enterprise workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier multi-core performance ranking in the top 10% of stress-ng benchmarks
